name: Roti Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour, baking soda, and salt into a bowl.
2. Add enough milk to form a stiff dough. Knead the dough well on a lightly floured board.
3. Form the dough into 4 or more equal-sized balls.
4. Roll out each ball of dough thinly to form 8-inch or 12-inch circles.
5. Brush each circle with a thin coating of vegetable oil.
6. Roll the dough back into balls, cover, and allow to stand for 15 minutes at room temperature.
7. Roll out the dough again and flatten to the original dimensions by patting lightly.
8. Heat a cast-iron or heavy-bottomed frying pan until it sizzles when tested with a drop of water.
9. Place one roti at a time in the pan and cook for about 1 minute, then turn and spread a thin layer of vegetable oil on the face of each roti.
10. Turn frequently until brown flecks appear on the surface. Remove from the frying pan.
11. Pound between the palms to make the roti supple.
12. Keep rotis warm and moist by covering with a towel.
Yields: 4-6 servings
